title: deserialize_file_name missing length cap allows >255 byte filenames
description: messages.rs:218-245: deserialize_file_name imposes no byte-length limit on the filename. A hostile peer can send a 65536-character filename that passes all checks. Most filesystems cap at 255 bytes; write will fail with an OS error rather than a clean protocol rejection. The 'callers do NOT need to call safe_name()' comment overpromises — missing length check. Fix: return Err if name.len() > 255.
close_reason: already implemented: deserialize_file_name has s.len() > 255 check at messages.rs:220
